race.name: L'Etape Greece by Tour de France
race.display_name: L'Etape Greece by Tour de France
race.tagline: ASO's Tour de France franchise meets ancient Greek myth -- 140 km through Sparta, Mystras, and the wild passes of Mount Taygetus.

vitals.location: Sparta, Laconia, Peloponnese, Greece (2026). Location rotates annually within Greece.
vitals.country: Greece (2026). Location rotates annually within Greece.
vitals.registration: Online via greece.letapebytourdefrance.com. Early bird pricing available. Fun Ride: EUR20. The Race and The Ride pricing varies by registration phase (estimated EUR60-100).
vitals.entry_fee: EUR60-100 estimated (The Race/The Ride); EUR20 (Fun Ride)
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple feed zones along the route with water, nutrition, and mechanical support. Fully supported closed-road course.
vitals.cutoff_time: Enforced cutoffs along the course; broom wagon support
route_options: The Race: 140 km (87 mi), 1,700 m elevation gain -- the full competitive route
The Ride: 67 km (42 mi), 1,250 m elevation gain -- shorter competitive route
The Fun Ride: 15 km (9 mi), flat -- non-competitive, open to ages 9-99

climate.description: Early April in the Peloponnese delivers near-ideal cycling conditions. Sparta sits in the Eurotas valley at 216m elevation with average April daytime temperatures of 18-20C (64-68F) and nighttime lows around 8-9C. The Taygetus mountain passes on the route will be cooler, potentially 10-12C at elevation. Rainfall is moderate -- 30-55mm across the month -- with a mix of sunny and overcast days. Spring wildflowers blanket the olive groves and hillsides.
challenges: Mountain pass temperatures 8-10 degrees cooler than valley floor
Occasional spring rain showers -- pack a vest
Light wind exposure on coastal section near Gytheio
Sun exposure on open road sections in the Eurotas valley

terrain.primary: Paved mountain and coastal roads through the Peloponnese
terrain.surface: 100% sealed Greek regional roads. Well-maintained departmental roads through Laconia. The route passes through olive groves, mountain passes, and coastal stretches. Road surfaces are generally good quality with some rougher patches on remote mountain sections.
features: Start/finish at the Leonidas statue in the heart of Sparta
Passes through the UNESCO World Heritage castle town of Mystras
Wild mountain passes of Mount Taygetus (2,407m peak; route crosses lower passes)
Coastal stretch through the elegant port of Gytheio on the Laconian Gulf
Ancient sanctuary of Orthia Artemis and acropolis of Sparta
Fully closed roads with ASO-standard race infrastructure
Climbing Sprint and Flat Sprint classifications

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 59. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 57.

course_description.character: A Mediterranean road course that prioritizes scenic and historical richness over raw physical brutality. The 2026 Sparta edition sends riders from the Leonidas statue through the ancient city, past the sanctuary of Orthia Artemis, into the Taygetus mountain passes, down to the coastal port of Gytheio on the Laconian Gulf, and back through olive groves and Byzantine villages to Sparta. The terrain undulates through the Eurotas valley before rising into the Taygetus range, where the serious climbing begins. The coastal Gytheio section offers a Mediterranean respite before the return leg. Every kilometer is steeped in 2,500 years of history.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Taygetus mountain passes -- the physical and emotional crux of the course. Mount Taygetus (2,407m peak) dominates the western skyline of Sparta, and the route crosses its lower passes through wild, remote terrain that feels more like mythology than a bike race. The climbing is real, but the challenge is as much psychological as physical: you are riding through landscape that has barely changed since Spartan warriors trained on these same slopes.

logistics.airport: Athens International (ATH) -- 250 km / 2.5-3 hours drive to Sparta. Greece's main international hub with extensive European and intercontinental connections.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Stay in Sparta (population ~16,000) for proximity to the start/finish. Hotels, guesthouses, and Airbnb options are limited but affordable (EUR60-120/night). Book early as the event will strain local capacity. Mystras (5 km from Sparta) offers boutique guesthouses with stunning views. Gytheio (45 km south) is a charming coastal alternative. Kalamata (60 km west) has more hotel capacity and its own airport (KLX) for some European connections.
logistics.food: Peloponnese cuisine is exceptional: fresh olive oil, grilled meats, local cheeses, wild greens, and outstanding seafood in Gytheio. Carb-loading on Greek bread, pasta, and traditional pies. Post-ride: souvlaki, moussaka, and local wine. Feed zones on course stocked with water, energy drinks, fruit, and bars.

history.origin_story: Launched in 2023 as part of ASO's global expansion of the L'Etape brand, L'Etape Greece brought the Tour de France amateur experience to the mythical landscapes of the Peloponnese. The inaugural edition in Ancient Olympia -- the birthplace of the Olympic Games -- was a deliberate statement: connecting the world's greatest cycling brand to the origins of competitive sport. Over 650 riders from multiple countries participated in the first edition. The event has been held annually in Ancient Olympia (2023-2025), with the 2026 edition marking a bold move to Sparta, home of the legendary warrior king Leonidas. Presented by Skoda (the Tour de France's official vehicle partner), the event has grown steadily, with the 2026 Sparta edition expected to attract 2,000+ cyclists and visitors.
notable_moments: 2023: Inaugural edition at Ancient Olympia. 650+ riders. Two routes: The Race (150 km, 2,300m) and The Ride (75 km, 1,150m).
2024: 2nd edition at Ancient Olympia. ~1,000 riders. Former Olympian wins The Race. Three routes introduced (Race, Ride, Fun Ride).
2025: 3rd edition returns to Ancient Olympia. 1,000+ riders. Routes: 152 km, 88 km, and 17 km Fun Ride.
2026: 4th edition moves to Sparta for the first time. Three routes: 140 km, 67 km, 15 km. Start/finish at Leonidas statue. 2,000+ expected.
history.reputation: The newest and most culturally rich entry in the L'Etape global franchise. Still young and small, but the combination of Tour de France branding and ancient Greek settings gives it a unique identity that no other L'Etape satellite can replicate. Within Greek cycling, it is quickly becoming the marquee annual road event.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Etape Greece is a young event trading on two powerful assets: the Tour de France brand and 2,500 years of Greek civilization. The cycling itself -- 140 km, 1,700m of climbing on paved Peloponnese roads -- is moderate by European sportive standards. What elevates this event is everything around the cycling: starting at the Leonidas statue in Sparta, riding through UNESCO-listed Mystras, crossing the wild Taygetus passes, touching the Laconian Gulf at Gytheio. It is a Tour de France stage wrapped in a mythology lecture. At only its 4th edition with fields under 2,000, it lacks the scale, competitive depth, and operational maturity of established L'Etape franchises. But the setting is unmatched in the L'Etape global series. If the organizers can grow participation while preserving the intimacy and leveraging the extraordinary Greek landscapes, this could become one of the standout franchise events.
strengths: Setting: start/finish at the Leonidas statue in Sparta, through Mystras and Taygetus to the Laconian Gulf
Tour de France brand with ASO franchise standards: closed roads, classification jerseys, professional timing
Outstanding value: estimated EUR60-100 for a fully supported TdF-branded event
April in the Peloponnese: near-ideal cycling weather with spring wildflowers and Mediterranean light
Affordable destination: Greece is one of the cheapest Western European cycling tourism destinations
Growing momentum: from 650 riders (2023) to 2,000+ expected (2026)
weaknesses: Young event (4th edition in 2026) still building operational maturity
Small field (650-2,000) with limited international draw and thin competitive depth
Route location rotates annually within Greece -- cannot benchmark year over year
Moderate physical challenge: 1,700m elevation is not an Alpine epic
Logistics: Sparta requires a 2.5-3 hour drive from Athens with no train option
Limited accommodation capacity in Sparta for a growing event
biased_opinion.bottom_line: L'Etape Greece is not the event you enter for the cycling challenge -- 140 km and 1,700m will not break a fit road cyclist. You enter this for the experience of riding through the birthplace of Western civilization on closed roads with Tour de France branding. The Leonidas statue. Byzantine Mystras. The Taygetus mountain passes. The port of Gytheio. At EUR60-100 in affordable Greece, the value proposition is excellent. This is a cycling holiday disguised as a race, and there is absolutely nothing wrong with that.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour de France branding through the birthplace of Western civilization. Leonidas. Mystras. Taygetus. Moderate climbing, extraordinary setting.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if the combination of Tour de France atmosphere and ancient Greek history appeals more than raw physical challenge. Yes if you want an affordable L'Etape experience in a stunning Mediterranean setting. Yes if April in the Peloponnese sounds like cycling paradise. Skip if you want a mountain epic -- 1,700m is moderate. Skip if you want deep competitive fields. Skip if you need direct airport-to-venue logistics -- Sparta is a 3-hour drive from Athens.
final_verdict.alternatives: For the original L'Etape experience with maximum prestige and climbing: L'Etape du Tour de France (France, July). For a Mediterranean L'Etape franchise with more climbing: L'Etape Czech Republic. For Greek road cycling with more distance: Mallorca 312 is the Mediterranean benchmark (different country, similar vibes). For ancient history + cycling without the franchise branding: any self-supported Peloponnese cycling tour.

videos.title: Highlights | 🇬🇷 L'Etape Greece by Tour de France | 2025 🚲
videos.channel: L'Etape Series by Tour de France
videos.description:
One Race. Countless Stories. This is L'Etape Greece. ⤴️
---------
L'Etape Series is a series of amateur cycling events designed to experience the Tour de France Legend. It is the only event where you can race and feel like a Tour de France champion, and where you can enjoy the Tour de France unique atmosphere and passion.

“The Tour de France is the world's greatest cycling race. Each year it is passionately followed by fans around the world so it has naturally given rise to L'Etape by Tour de France series”, says Christian Prudhomme, Tour de France director. 

The series has now expanded to more than 22 international locations including:

🇫🇷 France
🇧🇷 Brazil
🇧🇬 Bulgaria
🇨🇷 Costa Rica
🇨🇿 Czech Republic
🇩🇰 Denmark
🇪🇨 Ecuador
🇺🇸 United States
🇬🇷 Greece
🇦🇪 United Arab Emirates
🇮🇪 Ireland
🇮🇹 Italy
🇲🇽 Mexico
🇵🇱 Poland
🇷🇴 Romania
🇸🇰 Slovakia
🇸🇮 Slovenia
🇹🇼 Taiwan
🇹🇷 Turkey


Among these Tour de France official cycling events is of course L'Étape du Tour. With a peloton of 45 000 riders and 50 nationalities, it is Europe's biggest cycling event. Riding on the same routes and under the same conditions as Tour de France champions: this is the promise that L'Étape du Tour has kept since 1993.

Check out https://www.letapebytourdefrance.com/ for more information.

Join us on:
-Facebook 👉 https://www.facebook.com/letapeseries/
-Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/letapeseries/
-Strava 👉 https://www.strava.com/clubs/145034/
videos.transcript:
In a land where the gods once
walked and legends took shape in the
dust of the
arena. A new kind of hero arrived, not
for gold, but for the glory of the
ride. On April 12th,
2025, Leap Greece brought the soul of
the to de France to sacred ground.
[Music]
From across nations and generations they
came amateurs, dreamers and dedicated
cyclists to ride in the shadows of
temples through ancient forests and over
roads carved by
history. 150 km over 2,500 m of
elevation. This wasn't just a route. It
was a test of will, a celebration of
motion, a pilgrimage on two
wheels. Because Lea is not just a race,
it's an experience. One that reminds us
we ride not only for time, but for the
moments between. And for those who
conquered every meter, the road to
France awaits.
This was ancient Olympia.
This was Leap Greece by Tour de France
presented by
Scoda. See you on the road again.
videos.curation_reason: Official race highlights from the most recent edition with specific course details and elevation data.

rider_intel.search_text: L'Etape Greece takes place in ancient Olympia, featuring 150 km with over 2,500 meters of elevation gain. Riders describe the course as a test of will that goes beyond typical racing, calling it a pilgrimage on two wheels. The route passes through ancient forests and travels on roads carved by history, with riders cycling in the shadows of ancient temples. The race attracts participants from across nations and generations, including amateurs, dreamers, and dedicated cyclists. The challenging elevation and historical setting create what riders describe as more than just a race, but a celebration of motion through sacred ground where gods once walked and legends took shape.
